findings.message: Target PR #2 changed exactly the eight approved starter files and no extra files.
evidence: GitHub changed-file list returned README.md, index.html, package.json, src/data/templates.js, src/lib/safety.js, src/lib/status.js, src/main.js, and src/styles.css.
Target PR head SHA e3ab56ec0649e928418acc807c6badee2132521c matches the recorded build execution.

findings.message: The v1.1 upgrade preserves blocked live-action defaults across social OAuth, credentials, posting, scheduling, analytics, n8n, paid tools, and production deployment.
evidence: src/data/templates.js sets mode draft_only, approvalRequired true, livePostingBlocked true, schedulingBlocked true, analyticsApiBlocked true, socialOauthConnected false, credentialsStored false, and n8nAutomationBlocked true.

src/lib/safety.js asserts draft-only defaults and checks each account remains not_connected, draft_only, approvalRequired true, livePostingBlocked true, schedulingBlocked true, analyticsApiConnected false, credentialsStored false, and socialOauthConnected false.
src/main.js renders the Blocked Live Actions Panel and keeps export behavior local/static.

findings.message: The upgrade improves first-client readiness without creating active client records or real client data.
evidence: src/data/templates.js sets firstClientReadiness.status to intake_needed and activeClientRecordsCreated to false.
src/main.js shows Client Setup and Config Readiness sections using REQUIRED_ placeholders only.
The review found no hardcoded SurreyLinks, illustrative client, sample client, or real private client data.

findings.message: Safe local target checks passed without dependency installation or live service calls.
evidence: npm.cmd run check passed in the target branch temp clone.
npm.cmd test passed in the target branch temp clone.
npm.cmd run build passed and maps to the safe syntax check.
git diff --check passed.

findings.message: The target PR is ready for owner-approved merge after the review records are merged, but this critique cannot approve or perform the merge.
evidence: GitHub reports target PR #2 is open, unmerged, and mergeable.
Critique authority is advisory_only and blocksBuildMode is false.
A separate owner-approved target PR merge action remains required.

optionalImprovements: Run a browser responsive and accessibility review before broader owner-facing use.
Keep future social OAuth, analytics, scheduling, live posting, and n8n work behind separate approval packages.
After the target PR merge, redeploy only the dedicated Netlify staging-only site and record the deploy result.
